story.text: The gate/fence primitive (bdo's shape, this thread): we have *gateways* (policy -- they decide 'is this *actor* permitted?' from admitted who-may records: RBAC, a trust rung, the owner's stamp), but no deterministic *gate* that closes a gateway and no *fence* around the route, so the gateways are signs, not doors -- a policy 'deny' that nothing physical enforces. A gate and a fence are physical, not political, and must not be: 'you can see through it, you can't pass it, you can't climb it, and touching it bites.' The atom is a barrier-link -- a pure decide(link, act) that is deterministic, model-free, and ACTOR-BLIND: it reads only the act and the object (argv, the raw command, a path, an object flag), never who acts. Actor-blindness is the discriminator from a gateway and it is structural -- there is no predicate kind that can read the actor, and validate_link refuses any link that smuggles an authorization concept in. A fence is a CLOSED LOOP of barrier-links around a named territory; an open fence accomplishes nothing (you walk around it). validate_fence enforces bdo's physical adjectives as teeth: CLOSED across the front/seam/top route taxonomy (the meshed seams between links and the tall over-it route, not only the obvious front), BARBED (a blocked act is witnessed, never a silent deflection), and NOT-OPAQUE (every link carries a cold-reader reason). A gate is a barrier-link at a sanctioned opening in a fence. The S10 teeth bite on the real trunk-mutation territory: command_guard covers the front (git push) and the top (gh pr merge) but its quote-stripping leaves the SEAM open -- a `python -c "...subprocess...git push..."` slips it, because the git lives in the stripped-out quotes -- so our own git fence is proven TORN, sealed by one raw-command link. tests/test_barrier.py proves each law bites and that none is vacuous (1122-test suite green). Value: a deterministic vocabulary that makes a gateway's 'no' binding and rings its routes, distinguishes physical enforcement from political policy by a testable line (actor-blind), and on day one finds a real gap in our own perimeter. This is the CONTRACT; the installed fences (sealing the git seam, fencing the inference surface) are later increments -- instances of the primitive, not the primitive.
